trade.summary: Bilateral trade has collapsed to near-zero following Russia's invasion of Ukraine and comprehensive UK sanctions. Pre-war trade was already limited compared to continental European partners due to lower UK-Russia energy dependence. UK sanctions among the most extensive globally. Residual trade is in humanitarian and exempt categories only.
disputes: UK-led effort to seize Russian state assets for Ukraine reconstruction

diplomatic.summary: UK-Russia relations are at their worst since the Cold War and arguably worse in some respects. The Salisbury poisonings (2018), the invasion of Ukraine (2022), and persistent Russian espionage and disinformation operations have destroyed the relationship. The UK has been among the strongest supporters of Ukraine and harshest critics of Russia. UK expelled 23 Russian diplomats in 2018. Russia has designated the UK an 'unfriendly country.'
recent_incidents: GRU cyber attacks on UK infrastructure (2023-2025)
Russian state-sponsored disinformation campaigns in UK
UK seizure of Russian oligarch assets
Litvinenko murder (UK court found Putin responsible)

military.summary: The UK and Russia are in a state of de facto proxy conflict through Ukraine. The UK was the first country to supply Ukraine with long-range missiles (Storm Shadow). Russian nuclear threats directed at the UK. Russian GRU and SVR actively conduct espionage and hybrid warfare against the UK. All bilateral military cooperation terminated.
incidents: UK Storm Shadow missiles supplied to Ukraine targeting Russia
GRU Unit 29155 assassination attempts in UK
Russian submarine activity near UK undersea cables

financial.summary: UK froze billions in Russian oligarch assets. 'Londongrad' — the term for London's role as a home for Russian wealth — has largely been dismantled through sanctions and enforcement actions. UK banks have exited Russia. UK law firms and accountants no longer serve sanctioned Russian clients.

energy.summary: The UK was never highly dependent on Russian energy (unlike continental Europe). UK has fully eliminated Russian energy imports under sanctions. Some residual LNG trade continues through third-country intermediaries but is minimal and declining.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation effectively terminated. Russian researchers excluded from UK-funded programs. Individual contacts continue at very low levels through unofficial channels. Joint scientific programs in nuclear, space, and arctic research all suspended.
